text: $theFaction is perhaps just a legend to you, or maybe you haven't yet begun further interactions with these otherworldly visitors.

You've grown quite familiar with $theFaction. Stepping into these legends fills you with pride—at least you'll have more bragging rights among drunkards.
You know most of $theFaction's key figures, and the rest already know you. Interacting with these outsiders isn't as perilous as you once imagined.
The Crimson Lord seems fond of you, short-lived human that you are. Perhaps after tiring of Perseus' conflicts, retiring in this isolated fortress wouldn't be a bad choice?

script:
AddText "This place has almost no living beings except a few maids and men in suits. You start wondering how they maintain such vast space. The overly ornate decor feels alien to your pragmatic sensibilities, followed by an eerie emptiness." marketFlavorTextColor
AddText "A massive bordered metal door looms at the corridor's end. A long-eared maid informs you it's the Crimson Vault, where the Crimson Lord casually stores her disinterested treasures." marketFlavorTextColor

AddText "Even for a capital ship, its corridors are excessively wide. You encounter no crew—it seems stripped of combat capabilities, floating inertly as a makeshift habitat." marketFlavorTextColor

text:
"Not just humans~ But trust that Elis is never wrong—as you should trust me~" 
Seriously... that kill count in the records seems fabricated. 
Do I truly trust her? This sacred apparence may be a front, it does seem frivolous... 

"You're doubting my trustworthiness, aren't you?" 
"Or why I lack solemnity?" 

......Got it. 
She must have mind-reading magic. 

"Hmm... Be less predictable, Persean." 
"Rest assured—I can't read minds through screens." 

So... face-to-face it works?

text:

"You know... most divine doctrines come with troublesome rules..." 
"Would a being like me believe such contradictions?" 

So... she's a self-serving historical villain. 

"Yet humans cling to faith wherever they go~" 
"Whose cross shall I become this time?" 

...... 
First time sympathizing with Luddic faithfuls...

Were you even human before?
